response.task: Scope definition and effort estimation for digital restoration of 200 nitrate‑era films with varied conditions, including scheduling under vault constraints and grant milestones.

solution_steps.title: Create Master Catalog
actions: Compile a spreadsheet with fields: Title ID, Year, Base Type (nitrate or acetate), Condition Flags (vinegar syndrome, duplication artifacts, missing original negative), Source Archives, Required Restoration Tier.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: A 200‑row master list ready for planning.

solution_steps.title: Assign Restoration Tiers
actions: Tier A – Preservation Scan Only (30% of titles, mainly acetate with vinegar).
Tier B – Basic Digital Restoration (35% of titles, duplication negatives).
Tier C – Full 4K Restoration with Manual Frame‑by‑Frame Repair (35% of titles, including 12 reconstructed titles and remaining nitrate originals).
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Distribution: Tier A = 60 films, Tier B = 70 films, Tier C = 70 films.

solution_steps.title: Determine Scanning Protocols
actions: Nitrate base: 30 fps 4K, ~2.5 hrs per reel.
Acetate with vinegar: 20 fps 4K, ~3.8 hrs per reel (15% extra handling time).

solution_steps.title: Estimate Baseline Effort per Reel
actions: Tier A: 4 hrs per reel.
Tier B: 12 hrs per reel.
Tier C: 40 hrs per reel.

solution_steps.title: Add Overheads
actions: Vinegar handling: +15% to Tier A hours.
Duplication artifacts: +10% to Tier B hours.
2‑strip Technicolor grading: +5 hrs per reel for all tiers containing such prints.

solution_steps.title: Plan IP Clearance
actions: 40 orphan works, average 3 weeks each.
Schedule in 4‑week batches aligned with restoration phases to avoid blocking.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: IP clearance timeline integrated into Gantt chart.

solution_steps.title: Vault Acclimation Schedule
actions: 20 cans/day from 5°C vault to scanning suite.
10 days per batch, with a 5‑day buffer for scanner maintenance.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Daily workflow chart and buffer plan.

solution_steps.title: Build 24‑Month Gantt Chart
actions: Include restoration phases, scanner maintenance windows (2 weeks every 6 months), IP clearance blocks, and grant milestone dates.
Milestones: 6‑month deliverables – completed Tier A scans, 12‑month – initial Tier B restorations, 18‑month – progress on Tier C, 24‑month – final releases.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Visual timeline aligning work with funding disbursements.

solution_steps.title: Allocate Grant Funds
actions: First disbursement: after Tier A scans completion.
Second disbursement: upon delivery of initial Tier B restorations.
Third disbursement: at final release of Tier C titles.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Funding schedule matched to project milestones.

solution_steps.title: Validate Estimates
actions: Cross‑check against industry benchmarks (4K restoration 30–40 hrs/reel).
Adjust ±10% if necessary.

output.internal_reasoning: All figures derived from the problem statement percentages, standard scanning speeds for nitrate and acetate, typical labor hours per restoration tier, and industry benchmarks. Scheduling constraints (20 cans/day, 5‑day maintenance) were incorporated to produce a realistic Gantt chart. IP clearance was modeled with average durations to avoid blocking. The final plan aligns effort, schedule, and funding milestones.

Estimate effort for each restoration tier (preservation scan only, basic digital restoration, full 4K restoration with manual frame-by-frame repair), accounting for the variable scanning speed (acetate base vs nitrate base handling protocols), the specialized color grading expertise required for 2-strip Technicolor processes, and the intellectual property clearance process for 40 orphan works with uncertain rights holders. response.task: Scope definition and effort estimation for digital restoration of 200 nitrate-era films with varying conditions, including scheduling under vault constraints and grant milestones.

steps.action: Catalog all 200 titles by condition category (vinegar syndrome, duplication negatives, reconstruction cases) and assign unique IDs.
steps.result: A master spreadsheet with film ID, year, base type, condition flags, source archives, and required restoration tier.

steps.action: Determine scanning protocol for each base type: nitrate (high‑risk, 4K capable), acetate (vinegar syndrome, slower handling).
steps.result: Scanning speed table: Nitrate 30 fps at 4K (~2.5 hrs per reel), Acetate 20 fps (~3.8 hrs per reel).

steps.action: Allocate restoration tiers: preservation scan only (Tier A), basic digital restoration (Tier B), full 4K with manual repair (Tier C).
steps.result: Tier distribution: 30% Tier A, 35% Tier B, 35% Tier C based on condition severity.

steps.action: Estimate effort per tier using baseline hours: Tier A 4 hrs/reel, Tier B 12 hrs/reel, Tier C 40 hrs/reel.
steps.result: Total effort: Tier A 800 hrs, Tier B 1,200 hrs, Tier C 1,600 hrs.

steps.action: Add overhead for vinegar syndrome handling (extra safety checks + 15% time) and duplication artifacts (10% extra editing).
steps.result: Adjusted effort: Tier A 880 hrs, Tier B 1,320 hrs, Tier C 1,680 hrs.

steps.action: Incorporate color grading for 2‑strip Technicolor titles (estimated 5 hrs per reel).
steps.result: Additional 100 hrs added to Tier C effort.

steps.action: Plan intellectual property clearance: 40 orphan works, average 3 weeks each with legal review.
steps.result: IP window of 120 weeks; staggered by 4‑week batches to align with restoration phases.

steps.action: Schedule vault acclimation: 20 cans/day from 5 °C to scanning suite over 10 days per batch.
steps.result: Daily workflow chart showing 20 cans processed, buffer of 5 days for scanner maintenance.

steps.action: Create a 24‑month Gantt chart with scanner maintenance windows (2 weeks every 6 months).
steps.result: Timeline aligning restoration phases with maintenance and grant milestones.

steps.action: Allocate grant funds: disbursements every 6 months tied to deliverables (completed Tier A scans, first batch of Tier B restorations, final Tier C releases).
steps.result: Funding schedule with milestone KPIs and cost breakdown per phase.

steps.action: Validate all estimates against industry benchmarks (e.g., 4K restoration averages 30–40 hrs/reel).
steps.result: Confidence that effort numbers are realistic within ±10% variance.

task_reasoner.internal_reasoning: I broke the task into logical sub‑tasks: data cataloguing, protocol definition, tier assignment, effort estimation with overheads, color grading addition, IP clearance planning, vault acclimation scheduling, maintenance windows, grant alignment, and validation against benchmarks.
